req.title: Manager creates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates that an authenticated manager can create a timesheet via UI form with proper mandatory field validation, audit trail capture, and appropriate HTTP response codes. The suite covers the happy path (positive), missing mandatory fields and authentication failures (negative), and boundary conditions on field lengths and dates (boundary). Test design leverages Equivalence Partitioning for valid/invalid input classes, Boundary Value Analysis for field length limits, Decision Table logic for role/auth state combinations, and Negative Path coverage for error conditions.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_timesheet_success
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid session token
actions: POST /timesheets with valid payload: employee_id, start_date, end_date, hours (40), description
Query audit log for timesheet creation event
Retrieve created timesheet by response entity_id
expected: Audit record exists with action=CREATE, actor=manager_id, timestamp within 5 seconds
Timesheet record persists in database with submitted values
trace: R-WEB-017: authenticated manager shall be able to create a timesheet through the UI form
R-WEB-017: timesheet is created; audit record written; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_timesheet_success(api_client, auth_token_manager, db_session):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP001",
        "start_date": "2024-01-01",
        "end_date": "2024-01-07",
        "hours": 40,
        "description": "Regular work week"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_manager}"}
    resp = api_client.post("/timesheets", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data
    entity_id = data["id"]
    audit = db_session.query(AuditLog).filter(
        AuditLog.action == "CREATE",
        AuditLog.resource_id == entity_id
    ).first()
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.actor_id == "manager_id"
    ts = db_session.query(Timesheet).filter(
        Timesheet.id == entity_id
    ).first()
    assert ts.hours == 40
    assert ts.description == "Regular work week"

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid session token
actions: Verify response status and error message
Confirm no timesheet or audit record created
expected: Response body contains error message referencing missing 'hours' field
trace: R-WEB-017: Mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_unauthenticated
preconditions: No authentication token provided or token is expired
actions: POST /timesheets with valid payload but no Authorization header
expected: No timesheet or audit record created
trace: R-WEB-017: Precondition authenticated is not met
tests.executable:
def test_manager_create_timesheet_unauthenticated(api_client, db_session):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP001",
        "start_date": "2024-01-01",
        "end_date": "2024-01-07",
        "hours": 40
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/timesheets", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    count = db_session.query(Timesheet).count()
    assert count == 0

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_insufficient_role
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role=employee (not manager)
actions: POST /timesheets with valid payload using employee token
trace: R-WEB-017: Precondition role:manager is not met

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_boundary_max_hours
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid session token
System accepts hours field with maximum value of 168 (one week)
actions: POST /timesheets with hours set to boundary value 168
Verify response status and payload acceptance
Confirm timesheet created with correct hours value
expected: Timesheet record in database has hours=168
Audit record written with boundary value
trace: R-WEB-017: authenticated manager shall be able to create a timesheet through the UI form
R-WEB-017: Mandatory field validation for hours boundary condition
tests.executable:
def test_manager_create_timesheet_boundary_max_hours(
    api_client, auth_token_manager, db_session
):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "EMP001",
        "start_date": "2024-01-01",
        "end_date": "2024-01-07",
        "hours": 168,
        "description": "Maximum week hours"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_manager}"}
    resp = api_client.post("/timesheets", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data
    entity_id = data["id"]
    ts = db_session.query(Timesheet).filter(
        Timesheet.id == entity_id
    ).first()
    assert ts is not None
    assert ts.hours == 168
    audit = db_session.query(AuditLog).filter(
        AuditLog.action == "CREATE",
        AuditLog.resource_id == entity_id
    ).first()
    assert audit is not None
